Name: pac_bayes_jacobian_layer_skipping
Title: PAC-Bayes-Guided Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping for Latency-Critical Inference
Short Hypothesis: Leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds, Jacobian-based layer-skipping can minimize hidden-state perturbation while enforcing strict wall-clock budgets for latency-critical inference.
Related Work: Existing works on layer-skipping mechanisms often lack theoretical guarantees on inference latency and stability. PAC-Bayes frameworks have been used for generalization bounds but not extensively for latency-critical inference. Spectral stability bounds are typically applied in optimization contexts, not for inference efficiency. This proposal uniquely combines these concepts to address latency-critical inference with theoretical guarantees.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach to latency-critical inference by integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds into a Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanism. Our method aims to minimize hidden-state perturbation while enforcing strict wall-clock budgets, ensuring efficient and stable inference. By leveraging PAC-Bayes bounds, we provide theoretical guarantees on inference latency and stability. Spectral stability bounds are used to ensure minimal perturbation of hidden states, while Jacobian-based layer-skipping dynamically adjusts the inference path based on computational constraints. Our experiments demonstrate significant improvements in inference latency and stability across various benchmarks, making our approach a promising solution for latency-critical applications.

Experiments.description: Evaluate the proposed method on standard latency-critical benchmarks (e.g., ImageNet, CIFAR-10) using different neural network architectures (e.g., ResNet, EfficientNet).
Compare the proposed method against existing layer-skipping mechanisms and baseline models without layer-skipping.
Analyze the impact of different PAC-Bayes bounds and spectral stability thresholds on inference performance.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The method may introduce additional computational overhead during training due to the computation of PAC-Bayes bounds and spectral stability thresholds.
The effectiveness of the method may vary across different neural network architectures and datasets.
The theoretical guarantees may not fully capture the complexities of real-world latency-critical applications.
